metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop choreographic skills through the creation and refinement of original dance pieces. This course emphasizes artistic vision, movement vocabulary, and the translation of concepts into movement sequences.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on the principles and practice of creating original dance compositions.

big_ideas.statement: Choreography is a creative process that combines movement vocabulary, artistic intent, and compositional structures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Choreographers use various movement sources and compositional devices to develop coherent dance pieces that express ideas and emotions through movement.

big_ideas.statement: Dance movements can be organized and developed using various compositional techniques and thematic elements.
big_ideas.elaboration: Techniques such as repetition, variation, accumulation, and contrast shape how dance pieces are structured and perceived by audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Choreography connects to cultural contexts and personal expression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Dance pieces reflect cultural traditions, personal experiences, and contemporary perspectives, creating meaningful connections with audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Collaboration and feedback are essential to refining choreographic work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Working with dancers, peers, and mentors helps choreographers develop their vision and improve their craft through constructive critique and ensemble work.

competencies.raw: Explore choreographic processes and develop original movement sequences
i_can_statements: I can generate movement ideas using various movement sources and movement vocabulary
I can experiment with choreographic devices such as repetition, variation, and contrast
I can develop original dance sequences that are coherent and purposeful
I can refine movement material through repeated practice and exploration

competencies.raw: Create dances that communicate specific themes or artistic intent
i_can_statements: I can identify and articulate a creative intent or theme for my choreography
I can select and organize movement material that supports my artistic vision
I can create choreography that uses space, time, and energy effectively
I can document my choreographic process and explain my creative choices

competencies.raw: Explore various dance styles and their choreographic conventions
i_can_statements: I can identify and employ movement characteristics of different dance styles
I can experiment with blending different dance styles in my choreography
I can adapt choreographic conventions for my own artistic purposes
I can appreciate how different styles offer unique choreographic possibilities

competencies.raw: Develop choreography for various performance contexts
i_can_statements: I can create choreography suited to specific spaces, group sizes, or audiences
I can adapt choreography based on available resources and dancer abilities
I can consider practical constraints while maintaining artistic integrity
I can scale choreographic works appropriately for different contexts

competencies.raw: Analyze choreographic choices and their effects on audiences
i_can_statements: I can identify choreographic devices used in professional and peer dances
I can explain how specific movement choices create meaning or emotional impact
I can analyze how spatial relationships and formation changes affect audience perception
I can evaluate the effectiveness of choreographic structures in achieving artistic goals

competencies.raw: Reflect on personal choreographic development and artistic growth
i_can_statements: I can identify my strengths and areas for growth as a choreographer
I can set meaningful choreographic goals and work toward them systematically
I can reflect on how my artistic vision evolves through the creative process
I can analyze how feedback helps improve my choreographic work

competencies.raw: Connect choreography to cultural and historical contexts
i_can_statements: I can research and understand the cultural origins of specific dance styles
I can explain how historical events and cultural values influence choreography
I can recognize the contributions of diverse choreographers and dance traditions
I can incorporate cultural awareness into my own choreographic decisions

competencies.raw: Develop critical thinking about artistic decisions and alternatives
i_can_statements: I can evaluate different choreographic approaches to the same creative problem
I can make intentional artistic choices based on reasoned artistic judgment
I can explain the rationale behind my compositional structures
I can consider multiple perspectives when reflecting on choreographic work

competencies.raw: Document choreography through various notation and recording methods
i_can_statements: I can create clear written or visual documentation of choreographic sequences
I can use video recording to capture and review my choreography
I can organize and label choreographic material for easy reference
I can use notation systems or descriptive language to communicate movement

competencies.raw: Present choreography to audiences and dancers
i_can_statements: I can effectively teach my choreography to other dancers
I can communicate my artistic vision and creative intent to collaborators
I can give clear, respectful feedback to other choreographers
I can present my work in formal performance or sharing contexts

competencies.raw: Share choreographic ideas and creative process through discussion and reflection
i_can_statements: I can articulate my creative choices and explain my artistic reasoning
I can participate in collaborative discussions about choreographic development
I can respond constructively to peer feedback and suggestions
I can explain how my choreography connects to broader artistic or social themes
I can use my choreography to convey specific emotions or narratives
I can engage audiences through intentional movement expression
I can perform my own choreography with confidence and artistry
I can adapt my movement performance based on audience response and context

competencies.raw: Connect choreography to other art forms and disciplines
i_can_statements: I can integrate music, visual art, or spoken word into my choreography
I can explore how dance relates to mathematics, physics, or geometry
I can create interdisciplinary works that combine multiple artistic forms
I can understand how choreography complements other creative disciplines

competencies.raw: Explore contemporary and innovative choreographic approaches
i_can_statements: I can experiment with non-traditional movement vocabularies and sources
I can incorporate technology, props, or unconventional spaces into choreography
I can explore socially relevant themes through choreographic expression
I can develop a personal artistic voice that reflects contemporary concerns

competencies.raw: Engage with professional choreography and dance practices
i_can_statements: I can analyze and learn from professional choreographic works
I can understand the role of choreographers in dance companies and performance
I can explore career pathways in choreography and dance creation
I can attend and critically engage with live dance performances

competencies.raw: Build awareness of diverse choreographic traditions globally
i_can_statements: I can study choreographic traditions from various cultures and regions
I can respectfully incorporate elements from diverse dance traditions
I can recognize the unique contributions of choreographers from marginalized communities
I can use choreography as a vehicle for cross-cultural understanding

areas.name: Choreographic Devices and Structures
topics: Repetition, variation, contrast, and accumulation
Symmetry and asymmetry in movement organization
Canon and unison as compositional tools
Theme and variation development
Narrative and non-narrative structures
Space usage and formation changes

areas.name: Movement Vocabulary and Exploration
topics: Generation of movement material through improvisation
Exploration of body mechanics and movement qualities
Integration of various dance styles and techniques
Personal movement signature and artistic identity
Use of levels, directions, and pathways
Development of choreographic vocabulary

areas.name: Artistic Vision and Intent
topics: Identifying and articulating creative themes
Development of personal artistic voice
Expression of emotion and meaning through movement
Cultural and historical influences on choreography
Social and contemporary themes in dance
Personal narrative and lived experience in creation

areas.name: Collaboration and Performance
topics: Working with dancers as collaborators and interpreters
Teaching and communicating choreography to performers
Adaptation of choreography for different dancers and contexts
Performance considerations and staging
Feedback and revision processes
Documentation and archiving of choreographic work
